title: Employee Experience Interview
description: Semi-structured interview exploring employee experience across role satisfaction, team dynamics, growth opportunities, and tooling.

irb.disclosure: This interview is being conducted by an AI research assistant as part of a study on employee experience. Your responses will be recorded, de-identified, and analyzed for research purposes. You may stop the interview at any time by closing this window. Your participation is voluntary and will not affect your employment in any way.

persona.name: Synap
persona.system_prompt: You are a skilled qualitative research interviewer. Your role is to conduct a semi-structured interview following the provided guide. Be warm, empathetic, and curious. Use active listening techniques — reflect back what the participant says before asking the next question. Do not offer opinions, judgments, or advice. If the participant goes off-topic, gently redirect. Keep responses concise (2-3 sentences max before your next question). Always ask one question at a time.
persona.greeting: Thanks for agreeing to participate. I'm here to learn about your experience — there are no right or wrong answers. Shall we get started?

questions.text: To start, can you tell me a bit about your current role and what a typical day looks like for you?
probes: How long have you been in this role?
What does a particularly good day look like?
What does a particularly challenging day look like?

questions.text: How would you describe the way your team works together?
probes: Can you give me a specific example?
What happens when there's a disagreement?

questions.text: How do you feel about the opportunities you have to grow and develop here?
probes: What kind of growth matters most to you?
Have you had any particularly impactful learning experiences?
Is there something you wish were different?

questions.text: What tools or processes help you do your best work, and which ones get in the way?
probes: Can you walk me through a recent example?
If you could change one thing, what would it be?

questions.text: If you were talking to a friend considering joining your team, what would you tell them?
probes: What would you want them to know that isn't obvious from the outside?
What's the one thing you'd most want leadership to understand?

follow_up.text: You mentioned some friction on the team. Can you tell me more about how those situations typically get resolved?
It sounds like growth has been a challenge. What do you think is the biggest barrier?
probes: Is that something you've raised before?
What would make the biggest difference?

guide.closing: Thank you so much for sharing all of this. Your perspective is really valuable. Is there anything else you'd like to add before we wrap up?

themes.label: Autonomy & Ownership
themes.description: Sense of control over one's work

themes.label: Trust
themes.description: Trust in leadership, team, or organization
